reason: Changing the system-wide cryptography policy affects all services using the system crypto-policies framework, including SSH, TLS, and IPsec. While it is a standard hardening step, an incorrect policy selection can immediately break existing service connections, prevent remote SSH access, or cause application failures due to unsupported cipher suites.
estimated_impact: networking affected; Potential loss of SSH connectivity if the new policy disables current client/server ciphers; Service disruption for applications relying on specific legacy cryptographic algorithms; Potential breakage of automated deployment or management tools using TLS
